mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >65% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and construction activity, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge during early-cycle phases or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid month-over-month increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >60% of high-inflation months or the signal fails to predict returns when Fed policy is explicitly dovish.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in >50% of cases or the interaction effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects strong aggregate economic activity, which directly improves corporate profit margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: 5-day post-release returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero or negative in >55% of above-trend GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in the index of industrial production signal a contraction in manufacturing output and supply-side weakness, which reduces earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of double-dip industrial production months or the signal loses predictive power when global trade volumes are expanding.
